title: malus × robusta
intro: Malus × robusta, also known as the Robusta Crabapple, is a hybrid plant species derived from the Malus species, known for its attractive flowers and edible fruits. The Malus × robusta plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains a compound called amygdalin, a cyanogenic glycoside with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malus × robusta include its potential to aid in digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Malus × robusta can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
